AI/ML learning path

MLOps and production AI

End-to-end ML lifecycle management: experiment tracking, model serving, monitoring, CI/CD for ML, and infrastructure at scale.

Module progress 0%

0 of 3 lessons complete

Next up Lesson 1: ML pipeline design

Data pipelines, feature stores, training infrastructure, and experiment tracking for reproducible ML development.

Why this module matters Build one clean mental model before scaling it.

Move through the lessons in order so each trade-off has context before the harder distributed systems decisions show up.

Study target 3 lessons left

Aim to finish one lesson, then explain its core trade-off out loud before moving on.

AI Engineer learning path 3 lessons 0/3 complete Follow in order for best results Back to AI/ML roadmap Start module View lesson runway

Learning rhythm

Study this like a sequenced course, not a reference dump

  1. Preview the runway. Skim every lesson title first so you can see the progression before you dive in.
  2. Do one lesson deeply. Read the summary, inspect the diagram, and identify the sharpest trade-off.
  3. Say it back aloud. Explain the idea in interviewer language before you mark it complete.
  4. Only then advance. Move to the next lesson when you can defend the previous one without the notes open.
AI/ML sequence Designed for interview recall

Outcomes

What you should walk away with

  • Design ML pipelines from training through deployment and monitoring
  • Implement model serving with appropriate latency, cost, and reliability tradeoffs
  • Build monitoring systems that detect model drift and performance degradation

Practice flow

How to study each lesson

  • Read the summary and explain the core trade-off in your own words.
  • Study the diagram and identify the critical path or failure boundary.
  • Answer the interview prompts aloud before marking the lesson complete.

Mastery check

How you know it is sticking

  • Clear assumptions and estimated scale.
  • Justified component choices instead of pattern dumping.
  • Awareness of operations, failure modes, and trade-offs.

Lesson runway

Follow the sequence from first principles to fluency

This is the core pattern borrowed from stronger learning products: show the path, make the next step obvious, and keep outcomes visible while the learner moves.

Model serving and inference

Serving infrastructure, optimization techniques, scaling strategies, and cost management for production model inference.

ML monitoring and observability

25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections

Lesson 1

ML pipeline design

Open

Data pipelines, feature stores, training infrastructure, and experiment tracking for reproducible ML development.

Why it matters

Production ML requires reproducible pipelines. Ad-hoc notebooks do not scale to teams or production reliability requirements.

What you will cover

Data pipelinesTraining infrastructurePipeline orchestration

Good interview signal

  • Can design a reproducible training pipeline with proper versioning.
  • Understands feature store benefits for training-serving consistency.
30-40 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 2

Model serving and inference

Open

Serving infrastructure, optimization techniques, scaling strategies, and cost management for production model inference.

Why it matters

Serving is where ML models create business value. Latency, throughput, cost, and reliability directly impact user experience and unit economics.

What you will cover

Serving architecturesOptimization techniquesScaling and reliability

Good interview signal

  • Can choose the right serving architecture for latency and throughput requirements.
  • Applies optimization techniques to meet cost and latency budgets.
30-40 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson

Lesson 3

ML monitoring and observability

Open

Model drift detection, performance monitoring, alerting, and feedback loops for maintaining model quality in production.

Why it matters

Models degrade silently in production as data distributions shift. Without monitoring you discover failures from user complaints, not metrics.

What you will cover

What to monitorDrift detection methodsFeedback loops and retraining

Good interview signal

  • Monitors both technical metrics (latency, errors) and ML-specific metrics (drift, accuracy).
  • Has automated alerting for significant distribution shifts.
25-35 min 3 prompts 3 sections
Start lesson
